definition: A curious girl, a grinning cat, and a tea party that'll make you question reality.
Colorful pages where spandex-clad heroes and villains yell ‘Pow!’ and ‘Bam!’ for your delight.
A vampire novel that made capes, castles, and poor sleep schedules fashionable.
A sci-fi novel about giant worms, spice, and the best desert fashion statement ever.
A long story so grand, you might forget what real life is while reading.
A novel proving that building a person from spare parts creates paperwork and problems.
A tragic play where everyone overthinks, monologues, and makes terrible decisions.
Epic Greek poem of war, gods, and the famous heel that sparked a thousand battles.
A gothic novel of love, mystery, and an attic secret that might terrify your book club.
A 19th-century novel so epic it can double as a doorstop—and then sing about it.
A Shakespeare tragedy where ambition, witches, and bad choices ruin everyone’s evening.
A colossal whale tale proving revenge is best served with a side of sea salt.
Orwell’s classic that makes you eye your webcam suspiciously and question your dictionary.
An epic journey proving going home can take monsters, gods, and terrible navigation.
A Dickensian orphan who wants more gruel but ends up with more adventure.
A boy who refuses to grow up, raising serious property tax issues in Neverland.
The holy book of Islam that millions read, recite, and respect daily.
A gothic novel with more mansion drama than your average reality TV show.
A Stephen King novel that taught the world to fear hotels, axes, and twin girls in hallways.
A pirate adventure where maps, mutiny, and parrots make vacation planning dangerous.
